rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical epoxidation of styrene catalyzed by a Mn-based MOF (FICN-24) using bromide as a redox mediator in aqueous acetonitrile.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation cycles Mn(III)→Mn(IV)→Mn(V)=O at the FICN-24 MOF; bromide is co-oxidized to Br radical/Br2 which assists in forming a Mn(V)=O(Br) active species that transfers oxygen to the alkene.
electron_flow: At the anode, L-Mn(III)-OH is oxidized stepwise (−e−/−H+) to L-Mn(IV)=O and then to L-Mn(V)=O; concurrently Br− is anodically oxidized to Br•/Br2, which binds Mn to give an L-Mn(V)=O(Br) species that delivers oxygen to styrene, releasing the epoxide and regenerating L-Mn(III)-Br/OH with H2O.
rate_determining_step: Oxygen-atom transfer from L-Mn(V)=O(Br) to styrene C=C

probe_reagents.name: No-catalyst control
probe_reagents.observation: With NaBr but no FICN-24, 45% yield/45% selectivity — bromide mediation alone gives partial conversion, confirming MOF accelerates and improves selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: NaClO4 / TBAClO4 / TBACl
probe_reagents.observation: <1–18% yield; only Br− supports the catalytic cycle, ruling out generic anion effects.

probe_reagents.name: Anhydrous MeCN (no water)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% epoxide — water is required as the oxygen source for Mn=O regeneration.

notes: Mechanism is supported by CV showing Mn(III/IV) and Mn(IV/V) redox waves, in-situ Raman detecting Br-Br stretches (175, 269 cm−1) indicating Br2/polybromide formation, and in-situ FT-IR detecting an 880 cm−1 Mn-O-OH band plus C-O-C epoxide bands (1067, 1230 cm−1). Direct O-atom transfer from Mn(V)=O to alkene (via a putative Mn-oxo-alkene or radical-rebound) is proposed; an alternative purely Br•/Br2 (bromohydrin then base-closure) pathway is implicitly excluded because non-Mn controls give much lower selectivity.
